title: Traceability
requirement: All software components SHALL be traceable to defined system or software requirements and the corresponding Testcases and Reports.
verification_requirement: Each release candidate SHALL provide machine-readable traceability records linking software components to defined system or software requirements and corresponding test cases or reports.
automation_rationale: Traceability can be checked through ALM, repository, pipeline, and evidence links.

title: Source Code Integrity
requirement: All software source code SHALL be maintained in approved version control systems and changes SHALL be traceable to an identifiable author.
verification_requirement: Each repository SHALL provide machine-readable commit and author metadata showing that source code changes are maintained in an approved version control system and attributable to an identifiable author.
automation_rationale: Approved VCS and author traceability can be checked through repository metadata.

title: Source Code Integrity
requirement: Direct modification of protected branches SHALL be prohibited.
verification_requirement: Protected branch configuration SHALL show that direct pushes are disabled and changes require the defined integration workflow.
automation_rationale: Protected branch direct modification is objectively enforceable.

title: Secure Coding Practices
requirement: Secure coding practices SHALL be applied and security-relevant coding errors SHALL be detected during development or testing.
verification_requirement: Each releasable software change SHALL provide secure coding evidence, including configured static analysis results and code review records for the applicable technology stack.
automation_rationale: Secure coding cannot be fully judged automatically, but SAST and review evidence can be checked.

title: Software Supply Chain Transparency
requirement: All software dependencies SHALL be identifiable and documented.
verification_requirement: Each release candidate SHALL provide a machine-readable dependency inventory or SBOM covering the software dependencies included in the artifact.
automation_rationale: Dependency inventory can be checked through SBOM or dependency scan evidence.

title: Software Supply Chain Transparency
requirement: A Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) SHALL be generated for releasable artifacts.
verification_requirement: Each release candidate SHALL provide a machine-readable SBOM linked to the releasable artifact identifier.
automation_rationale: SBOM presence and artifact linkage can be enforced for release candidates.

title: Controlled Build Process
requirement: Software builds SHALL be executed through controlled automated pipelines.
verification_requirement: Each release candidate SHALL provide pipeline execution evidence showing that build activities were executed through controlled automated pipelines.
automation_rationale: Pipeline execution evidence can prove automated builds.

title: Controlled Build Process
requirement: Build outputs SHALL be uniquely identifiable.
verification_requirement: Each build output SHALL provide artifact metadata containing a unique artifact identifier, version, digest, or equivalent repository identifier.
automation_rationale: Artifact identifiers can be checked through artifact metadata.

title: Vulnerability Identification
requirement: Automated vulnerability scanning SHALL be performed during build or test stages.
verification_requirement: Each build or release candidate SHALL provide machine-readable vulnerability scan evidence from the build or test stage.
automation_rationale: Vulnerability scan presence can be enforced in the pipeline.

title: Vulnerability Identification
requirement: Identified vulnerabilities SHALL be evaluated prior to release.
verification_requirement: Each release candidate SHALL provide vulnerability assessment evidence showing that identified vulnerabilities were evaluated or covered by an approved waiver before release.
automation_rationale: Release can be blocked for unacceptable findings without approved waiver.

title: Artifact Integrity
requirement: Releasable software artifacts SHALL be protected against tampering by approved repository integrity controls and cryptographic integrity mechanisms such as checksums, digests, or signatures, as applicable to the required control baseline level.
verification_requirement: Each releasable artifact SHALL provide integrity evidence such as checksum, digest, repository integrity metadata, or signature linked to the artifact identifier.
automation_rationale: Artifact digest, checksum, or signature evidence can be enforced.

title: Artifact Integrity
requirement: Artifacts SHALL be uniquely identifiable.
verification_requirement: Each artifact SHALL provide repository metadata proving a unique and stable artifact identity.
automation_rationale: Artifact identity can be checked through repository metadata.

title: Release Authorization
requirement: Deployment of software artifacts SHALL require authorized approval.
verification_requirement: Each deployment or release candidate SHALL provide approval evidence linked to the artifact, release, or deployment record.
automation_rationale: Release approval evidence can be checked through workflow or environment approvals.

title: Release Authorization
requirement: Only approved artifacts SHALL be deployed.
verification_requirement: Deployment evidence SHALL show that only artifacts with approved release status were deployed.
automation_rationale: Deployment can be blocked when artifact approval evidence is missing.

title: Pipeline Evidence Generation
requirement: DevSecOps pipelines SHALL generate machine-readable evidence artifacts for implemented controls.
verification_requirement: Each pipeline execution SHALL produce a machine-readable evidence bundle covering the implemented controls applicable to the pipeline stage.
automation_rationale: Machine-readable evidence bundle presence can be checked.

title: Operational Traceability
requirement: Operational environments SHALL maintain records of deployed software versions and relevant security events.
verification_requirement: Each operational deployment SHALL provide records linking deployed software versions to deployment records and relevant security or incident events.
automation_rationale: Operational version and security event records can be checked through deployment and logging integrations.

title: Secure Development Environments
requirement: Development environments SHALL be centrally managed.
verification_requirement: Platform inventory or configuration management evidence SHALL show that development environments are centrally managed.
automation_rationale: Central management evidence can be checked through endpoint or platform inventory.

title: Secure Development Environments
requirement: Development environments SHALL comply with enterprise security configuration standards.
verification_requirement: Development environment evidence SHALL show compliance with the applicable enterprise security configuration baseline.
automation_rationale: Configuration baseline compliance can be checked through endpoint/configuration evidence.

title: DevSecOps Platform Access Control
requirement: Access to DevSecOps platforms SHALL be controlled through centralized identity management
verification_requirement: Platform configuration evidence SHALL show that DevSecOps platform access is integrated with centralized identity management.
automation_rationale: Central identity management can be checked through platform configuration.

title: DevSecOps Platform Access Control
requirement: Multi-factor authentication SHALL be enforced for privileged access.
verification_requirement: Privileged access evidence SHALL show that multi-factor authentication is enforced for privileged DevSecOps platform roles.
automation_rationale: Privileged access without MFA can be detected and blocked.

title: Dependency Source Control
requirement: Software dependencies SHALL be retrieved from approved repositories.
verification_requirement: Build and dependency configuration SHALL show that software dependencies are retrieved from approved repositories.
automation_rationale: Approved dependency repository use can be enforced in build configuration.

title: Dependency Source Control
requirement: Direct downloads from external sources SHALL NOT be permitted.
verification_requirement: Pipeline, build, or dependency logs SHALL show that direct downloads from non-approved external sources are not used.
automation_rationale: Direct external downloads can be detected in pipelines and build logs.

title: Artifact Signing
requirement: Software artifacts SHALL be cryptographically signed before release.
verification_requirement: Each releasable artifact SHALL provide signature evidence created before release.
automation_rationale: Unsigned artifacts can be blocked before release.

title: Artifact Signing
requirement: Signing keys SHALL be protected and managed according to enterprise security policies.
verification_requirement: Signing infrastructure evidence SHALL show that signing keys are protected and managed according to enterprise security policy.
automation_rationale: Key protection requires evidence from key management or signing infrastructure.

title: Infrastructure as Code
requirement: Infrastructure used for software deployment SHALL be defined through Infrastructure as Code.
verification_requirement: Deployment evidence SHALL reference version-controlled Infrastructure as Code definitions for the deployed infrastructure.
automation_rationale: IaC presence can be checked through repository and deployment metadata.

title: Infrastructure as Code
requirement: Infrastructure definitions SHALL be maintained in version control.
verification_requirement: Infrastructure definition evidence SHALL show that IaC sources are maintained in version control with change history.
automation_rationale: Version control for IaC can be checked through repository metadata.

title: Pipeline Security Gates
requirement: DevSecOps pipelines SHALL enforce security gates.
verification_requirement: Pipeline execution evidence SHALL show that required security gates were executed for the relevant lifecycle stage.
automation_rationale: Security gate execution can be enforced in pipeline templates.

title: Pipeline Security Gates
requirement: Releases SHALL NOT proceed when defined security thresholds are exceeded.
verification_requirement: Release decision evidence SHALL show that releases are blocked when security thresholds are exceeded unless an approved waiver exists.
automation_rationale: Release can be blocked when thresholds are exceeded without waiver.

title: Security Monitoring
requirement: Operational systems SHALL generate security-relevant events.
verification_requirement: Operational system evidence SHALL show that defined security-relevant events are generated.
automation_rationale: Security event generation can be checked through logging configuration or telemetry evidence.

title: Security Monitoring
requirement: Security events SHALL be forwarded to monitoring systems.
verification_requirement: Monitoring configuration or telemetry evidence SHALL show that security events are forwarded to approved monitoring systems.
automation_rationale: Forwarding to monitoring can be checked through logging/monitoring configuration evidence.

title: Deterministic Builds
requirement: Software builds SHALL be reproducible using identical source code and build configuration.
verification_requirement: Rebuild verification evidence SHALL show that the same source code and build configuration can reproduce the expected build output within defined tolerance.
automation_rationale: Reproducible build verification can be automated after deterministic build tooling is established.

title: Deterministic Builds
requirement: Build configurations SHALL be version controlled.
verification_requirement: Build configuration evidence SHALL show that build definitions are maintained in version control and linked to the build execution.
automation_rationale: Build configuration version control can be checked through repository metadata.

title: Build Environment Isolation
requirement: Software builds SHALL be executed in isolated build environments.
verification_requirement: Pipeline evidence SHALL show that builds are executed in isolated build environments.
automation_rationale: Build environment isolation can be checked through runner/container evidence.

title: Build Environment Isolation
requirement: Build environments SHALL be recreated for each pipeline execution.
verification_requirement: Pipeline environment evidence SHALL show that build environments are recreated for each controlled pipeline execution.
automation_rationale: Recreated build environments can be checked through pipeline environment metadata.

title: Dependency Provenance
requirement: Software dependencies SHALL include verifiable provenance information.
verification_requirement: Dependency metadata SHALL provide verifiable provenance information for dependencies used in the build.
automation_rationale: Dependency provenance requires provenance-capable tooling and metadata.

title: Dependency Provenance
requirement: Dependency provenance SHALL be recorded during the build process.
verification_requirement: Build evidence SHALL record dependency provenance metadata during the build process.
automation_rationale: Build-time provenance recording requires provenance capture tooling.

title: Trusted Artifact Signing
requirement: Software artifacts SHALL be signed using enterprise signing infrastructure.
verification_requirement: Artifact signature evidence SHALL show that released artifacts are signed using enterprise signing infrastructure.
automation_rationale: Enterprise signing infrastructure can enforce artifact signing.

title: Trusted Artifact Signing
requirement: Signing keys SHALL be centrally managed.
verification_requirement: Trust infrastructure evidence SHALL show that signing keys are centrally managed.
automation_rationale: Central key management is evidenced by signing infrastructure configuration.

title: End-to-End Traceability
requirement: Traceability SHALL exist between requirements, source code, verification Cases / Procedures and Test result, build artifacts, and deployed software.
verification_requirement: Each release candidate SHALL provide end-to-end traceability evidence linking requirements, source code, verification results, build artifacts, deployment metadata, and deployed software.
automation_rationale: End-to-end traceability can be graph-checked after ALM/repo/pipeline integration.

title: Runtime Integrity Verification
requirement: Runtime environments SHALL verify the integrity of deployed software artifacts.
verification_requirement: Runtime evidence SHALL show integrity verification results for deployed software artifacts.
automation_rationale: Runtime integrity verification requires runtime attestation or verification telemetry.

title: Continuous Compliance Evidence
requirement: DevSecOps platforms SHALL generate machine-readable compliance evidence for lifecycle activities.
verification_requirement: The DevSecOps platform SHALL provide machine-readable compliance evidence covering lifecycle activities and linked control identifiers.
automation_rationale: Continuous compliance evidence can be checked through evidence repository completeness.

title: Applicability
requirement: All programs SHALL implement the Level 1 Control Baseline.
verification_requirement: Program governance records SHALL identify the applicable Control Baseline level for each program.
automation_rationale: Program baseline assignment can be checked through governance records.

title: Compliance Verification
requirement: Compliance with this Standard SHALL be verified through:
verification_requirement: Compliance verification evidence SHALL include maturity assessment, audit, architecture review, or program lifecycle review records as applicable.
automation_rationale: Compliance verification includes audit and review activities that require human judgment.

title: Compliance Verification
requirement: Non-compliance requires an approved waiver.
verification_requirement: Non-compliance records SHALL reference an approved waiver before a release, deployment, or governance decision is accepted.
automation_rationale: Non-compliance without approved waiver can be blocked in governance/release checks.

title: Deviations
requirement: Deviations from this Standard SHALL follow the waiver process defined in the DevSecOps Directive.
verification_requirement: Deviation records SHALL reference the defined waiver process and waiver registry entry.
automation_rationale: Deviation process use can be checked through waiver registry records.

title: Deviations
requirement: All deviations SHALL:
verification_requirement: Each deviation SHALL provide machine-readable waiver evidence containing justification, documentation reference, risk classification, and expiry date.
automation_rationale: Waiver completeness and expiry can be automatically checked.
